matched_ingredients.name: Oat Milk
matched_ingredients.reason: Its gentle roastiness and natural sweetness bridge the gap between the espresso base and floral lavender notes.

matched_ingredients.name: Lavender Syrup
matched_ingredients.reason: High brightness (8/10) cuts through cream and coffee weight, giving the drink an unexpected floral lift.

matched_ingredients.name: Espresso Shot
matched_ingredients.reason: Roastiness (9/10) and bitterness anchor the drink, preventing the lavender and vanilla from turning cloying.

matched_ingredients.name: Vanilla Syrup
matched_ingredients.reason: Warmth (5/10) and sweetness round out the finish, making the floral notes approachable for all palates.

recipe_steps: Pull 1 double espresso shot into a 12oz cup.
Add 2 pumps lavender syrup and 1 pump vanilla syrup directly over the espresso. Stir briefly.
Steam 6oz oat milk to 140°F, aiming for a microfoam with a light, silky texture.
Pour steamed oat milk slowly over the espresso-syrup base.
Garnish with a single dried lavender sprig. Serve immediately.

creative.input: something warm and mysterious, like a rainy afternoon in a library with old wooden shelves

matched_ingredients.name: Espresso Shot
matched_ingredients.reason: Roastiness (9/10) and bitterness provide the deep, slightly dark backbone a 'rainy library' atmosphere demands.

matched_ingredients.name: Cardamom
matched_ingredients.reason: High complexity (9/10) and brightness (7/10) add the unexpected aromatic quality of old paper and polished wood.

matched_ingredients.name: Brown Sugar Syrup
matched_ingredients.reason: Warmth (8/10) and gentle roastiness echo aged oak and amber candlelight without pushing the drink toward dessert.

matched_ingredients.name: Oat Milk
matched_ingredients.reason: Medium creaminess (6/10) softens the bitter-spice edge, the way a wool blanket softens a cold afternoon.

matched_ingredients.name: Cocoa Powder
matched_ingredients.reason: Adds a whisper of roasty bitterness (7/10) and complexity that deepens the drink without fully revealing itself.

recipe_steps: Pull 1 double espresso shot into a warmed 10oz cup.
Stir in 2 pumps brown sugar syrup and a 1/4 tsp cardamom until dissolved.
Steam 5oz oat milk to 150°F — a full, velvety microfoam.
Pour the steamed oat milk over the espresso base in a slow, deliberate stream.
Dust the foam surface with a fine layer of cocoa powder using a fine-mesh sieve.
Rest for 10 seconds. Let the aromas gather. Then serve.
